source: AXIS Foil Riders FB Group + AXIS Foils Official Page

posts_in_window.text: Catching waves with lower motor pod in efoil position on axis integrated mast using fusion. Still can pump and stay on the bumps. Small 1-2ft shore break type of day.. but nice conditions for filming and had friends wanting to go out. Local wakesurf and first person to have a foil named after them (Austin keen wake model by liquid force for wake in 2017?) also came to rip the shore break (footage at the end). Got tired of waiting for sets so half way through decided to put the efoil mast on to rip around flat water while waiting for waves. Been catching waves and boat wakes with mt lift efoils for over 6 years now and always surprised to hear people think it's not possible to do with motor drag. It's fairly easy with some good coaching and practice.

comments.author: Bardy Matias
comments.text: Can you feel a lot of drag tho? I've never even considered tryna ride motor off? You think a Stoke Boost would be similar?

comments.author: William Vosburgh
comments.text: What fuse? Water looks nice and warm

comments.author: Carlo Prado S
comments.text: That is up in the lagoons up in the highlands, Andes. Water definitely is not warm.lol

posts_in_window.text: Axis/Amos Nitro tip - I recently bought an Amos Nitro for prone and noticed straight away it didn't feel right. Mast pushed forward was way too much lift (Surge890) and twitchy as hell. Moving the mast back I noticed the Nitro tapers off (rocker) which causes the board to run nose high when on foil. This also affects takeoffs, pumping and makes foot position unforgiving. The Answer: 1deg Mast shim! Now takeoffs are smooth and predictable, foot position is forgiving, most importantly the board is level when on foil and everything now feels amazing as it should!

comments.author: Liam Walton
comments.text: I sold mine after 2 sessions. Mast tracks are too far forward and the board has built in 1° angle difference between the mast tracks and the top so it will always ride nose up. I don't think I even got it up and foiling

comments.author: Mike Holland
comments.text: Yep in the early days of foiling, boards still had some tail rocker, it sucked!

posts_in_window.title: Fatty Mast and foil drive!
posts_in_window.text: Hi legends, Any power athletes at 100kg or about have experience with the fatty mast + fuse with a foil drive, max or fusion they could share please? Thanks Mick.

comments.author: Mark Shinn
comments.text: Not sure I am a power athlete but I am around that weight and have extensive experience with the AXIS Foils Fatty mast and fuse! I have used it with the Fusion, Max and Slim..... not sure the unit you bolt it too makes any difference. The Universal pod...

comments.author: Jimmy Jam Foils
comments.text: Deon Aumaier has a fatty and Foildrives with it I believe. Using 1750

posts_in_window.text: New name, same data. Enjoy! www.foildata.app

comments.author: Stephen Westwood
comments.text: The submit review isn't working: Something went wrong. Please try again.

posts.text: We surveyed nearly 150 AXIS riders across 15+ countries to find out what they're riding, how they set it up, and what they'd change. The results are in, and some of them surprised us. Want your setup counted? Takes 3 minutes. Top Disciplines: 1. Wing Foiling 2. Downwind SUP 3. Parawing 4. Prone. Who's Riding: Mostly 65-85 kg. 60% foiling 4+ years. Average rider does 3+ disciplines. Top Wing Models: Surge · Fireball · Spitfire. Top Regions: US West Coast · NZ/Australia · Europe. 'Turns off the back foot like surfing. The Surge is a game changer.'

comments.author: Katrin Kolo
comments.text: Thanks for this survey. I answered already and would be interested what percentage of answers is female riders (in each discipline if possible and especially dockstarting). Thanks!

comments.author: AXIS Foils (Official Reply)
comments.text: Just pulled fresh from the survey (now at 241 responses): Long Distance Pump: 2 out of 45 riders are female → 4.4%. Dock Start: 2 out of 67 riders are female → 3.0%. Pretty thin representation in both disciplines. Worth noting 20-22% didn't answer the gender question though, so actual numbers could be slightly higher.

posts.text: When we were working on product visuals, colorways, surface treatment, and textures on the Surge front wings, we kept on coming back to desert environments...
It's all happening in the pool at @bootduesseldorf for the @surffoilworldtour. Stoked to see our team getting amongst the good times and podiums
